title: Create OrderApi .NET 8.0 project with Order model
description: Create OrderApi project following UserApi/ProductApi patterns. Order model: Id, UserId, ProductIds (JSON array), Total, Status, CreatedAt

title: Add Orders table to database schema
Add Orders feature: cart and checkout flow
description:
Users can browse products, add them to a cart, checkout to create an order, and view their orders on the dashboard.

Acceptance Criteria:
- User can select a user from dropdown (simulating login)
- User can add products to cart
- User can view cart with running total
- User can checkout, creating an order
- Order appears on dashboard with status
- Order persists in database

This epic breaks down into: contract definition, backend implementation, frontend implementation, and integration testing.

title: Define Orders API contract
description:
Define the API contract for the Orders feature before implementation begins.

Deliverable: Create api/OrderApi/orders-contract.yaml (or similar) defining:

Endpoints:
  POST /api/orders
    Request: { userId: int, productIds: int[], quantities: int[] }
    Response: { orderId: int, total: decimal, status: string, createdAt: datetime }

  GET /api/orders
    Query: ?userId={id}
    Response: [ { orderId, userId, total, status, createdAt } ]

  GET /api/orders/{id}
    Response: { orderId, userId, items: [{ productId, name, price, quantity }], total, status, createdAt }

Models:
  Order: Id, UserId, Total, Status (Pending/Confirmed/Shipped), CreatedAt
  OrderItem: Id, OrderId, ProductId, Quantity, UnitPrice

Database:
  Orders table, OrderItems table with foreign keys

This contract is the source of truth for both frontend and backend implementation.

title: Implement Orders API backend
description:
Implement the Orders API backend per the contract defined in fullstack-test-app-2xx.1.

Reference: Read the contract at api/OrderApi/orders-contract.yaml

Implementation:
1. Create api/OrderApi/ project (similar to UserApi/ProductApi)
2. Models:
   - Order.cs: Id, UserId, Total, Status, CreatedAt
   - OrderItem.cs: Id, OrderId, ProductId, Quantity, UnitPrice
3. OrdersController with endpoints per contract:
   - POST /api/orders (create order)
   - GET /api/orders?userId={id} (list user orders)
   - GET /api/orders/{id} (order details with items)
4. Database:
   - Add Orders and OrderItems tables to db/init/
   - EF Core DbContext configuration
5. Infrastructure:
   - Add to docker-compose.yml (port 5002)
   - Add nginx routing: /api/orders/* -> OrderApi

Push branch when complete: git push origin HEAD

Validation: API returns responses matching contract shapes.

title: Implement Orders UI frontend
description:
Implement the Orders UI frontend per the contract defined in fullstack-test-app-2xx.1.

Reference: Read the contract at api/OrderApi/orders-contract.yaml

Implementation:
1. Services:
   - order.service.ts: API calls matching contract endpoints
   - cart.service.ts: Local cart state management
2. Components:
   - shopping/: Product list with Add to Cart buttons
   - cart/: Cart panel showing items, quantities, total
   - checkout/: Checkout flow, calls POST /api/orders
   - order-history/: List of user orders
   - order-detail/: Single order view with items
3. Routing:
   - /shopping: Browse and add to cart
   - /cart: View cart
   - /orders: Order history
   - /orders/:id: Order detail
4. State:
   - Selected user (dropdown, simulates login)
   - Cart contents (local state)
5. Integration with existing:
   - Add nav links for Shopping, Orders
   - Use existing user dropdown or add one

Push branch when complete: git push origin HEAD

Validation: UI makes correct API calls matching contract shapes.

title: Integrate and validate Orders feature
description:
Merge backend and frontend branches, resolve any integration issues, and validate the complete Orders feature.

Prerequisites:
- Backend branch pushed (from fullstack-test-app-2xx.2)
- Frontend branch pushed (from fullstack-test-app-2xx.3)

Integration Steps:
1. Fetch latest branches:
   git fetch origin
2. Create integration branch:
   git checkout -b orders-integration
3. Merge backend:
   git merge origin/tsk/task/implement-orders-api-backend-* --no-edit
4. Merge frontend:
   git merge origin/tsk/task/implement-orders-ui-frontend-* --no-edit
5. Resolve any merge conflicts
6. Start services: docker compose up -d --build
7. Run E2E validation:
   - Create a test order via UI
   - Verify order appears in database
   - Verify order shows in order history
   - Check API responses match contract

Integration Outcomes:
- IDEAL: No conflicts, E2E passes → close epic as complete
- SUB-OPTIMAL: Minor fixes needed (typos, import issues) → fix inline, complete
- FAILURE: Structural incompatibility → document issues, create fix tickets

On success: Push integration branch, close this ticket and parent epic.

title: Add CRUD endpoints for Orders
description: Implement GET/POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints in OrdersController following existing API patterns

title: Add Orders API backend
description:
Create OrderApi (.NET 8.0 Web API) microservice:

Backend Components:
- Order model (Id, UserId, ProductIds, Total, Status, CreatedAt)
- OrdersController with CRUD endpoints
- Database schema for Orders table
- EF Core DbContext and migrations

Infrastructure:
- Add OrderApi service to docker-compose.yml
- Configure nginx gateway routing (/api/orders/* → OrderApi)
- Add OrderApi port (5002) to architecture

Follow existing patterns from UserApi and ProductApi.

title: Add navigation bar
description:
Add Material navigation bar with:
- Dashboard link (shows users/products management)
- Shopping link (shows shopping interface)
- Use Material toolbar and sidenav components
- Update routing for new pages

title: Add Orders feature
description:
Add complete Orders functionality:
- OrderApi (.NET 8.0) with Order model (Id, UserId, ProductIds, Total, Status, CreatedAt)
- CRUD endpoints following existing API patterns
- Database schema for Orders table
- Update docker-compose and nginx gateway
- Angular components: order list, create form, detail view

title: Add Angular Material
description:
Install and configure Angular Material:
- Install @angular/material and @angular/cdk
- Add a Material theme
- Convert existing UI components to use Material components (cards, buttons, tables, forms)
- Update dashboard, users, and products components with Material styling

title: Add shopping and orders flow
description:
Complete shopping experience (frontend only - uses Orders API from fullstack-test-app-6lc):

Frontend (Shopping page):
- User dropdown to select current user
- Product list with 'Add to Cart' buttons  
- Cart sidebar/panel showing selected items
- Checkout button that creates an order via OrderApi

Frontend (Dashboard):
- Orders section showing recent orders for selected user
- Order details (items, total, status, date)

title: Convert CSS to SCSS
description:
Convert all CSS files to SCSS:
- ui/src/styles.css → styles.scss
- ui/src/app/app.css → app.scss  
- ui/src/app/components/dashboard/dashboard.css → dashboard.scss
- ui/src/app/components/users/users.css → users.scss
- ui/src/app/components/products/products.css → products.scss
- Update angular.json to use SCSS
- Update component decorators to reference .scss files

title: Add Shopping Cart with Checkout
description:
Add shopping cart functionality:
- CartApi or extend existing APIs with cart endpoints
- Cart model (Id, UserId, Items, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt)
- Add to cart, remove from cart, update quantity
- Checkout flow that creates an Order from cart items
- Angular components: cart view, cart icon with count, checkout page
